storyBeat: 兔子愛現,烏龜怎敢挑戰?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的開場單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 fast = 快
🔑 slow = 慢
🔑 laugh = 笑
🔑 field = 田野
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every day, the hare jumped fast across the open field.
questions.explanationZh: 每天，兔子都在田野上嗖嗖嗖地跳，風都跟不上他，跑得可神氣了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Look at me! I am the fastest!" he said loud to the other animals.
questions.explanationZh: 「看我！我最快！」他昂著頭大聲說，聲音響遍整個田野，讓所有動物都聽見。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal said a word back, and they all looked at the ground.
questions.questionEn: Did the other animals enjoy his words?
questions.explanationZh: 沒人回應、個個低頭——誰喜歡聽人一直吹噓？動物們早就聽膩了，所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 沒有任何動物回話，大家都低著頭看地。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A tortoise walked by, slow and quiet, on the side of the path.
questions.explanationZh: 就在這時候，一隻烏龜慢慢地、安靜地走過來，低著頭，穩穩地沿著路邊走。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Step by step, his short legs moved without any sound.
questions.question: How did the tortoise move?
questions.explanationZh: 一步一步無聲地走——不快、不急，就是「又慢又安靜」。
questions.sentenceZh: 他短短的腿，一步一步，沒有一點聲音。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Hello, slow one," the hare laughed. "Can you even walk?"
questions.explanationZh: 「嗨，慢慢仔！」兔子湊過去笑他，「你這樣也叫走路？」語氣充滿嘲弄。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the hare treat the tortoise?
questions.question: How did the hare treat him?
questions.explanationZh: 兔子笑著問「你會走路嗎」，這不是關心，就是「笑他」。在大家面前這樣說，真不好意思。
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子是怎麼對待烏龜的？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare bent down close to the tortoise's small head, still smiling.
questions.question: How close was the hare to the tortoise?
questions.explanationZh: 「彎下身靠近」——距離這麼近，當然是「很近」，幾乎鼻子都碰到了。
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子彎下身，湊近烏龜的小腦袋，臉上還掛著笑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big rabbit teased the small green animal in front of the whole field.
questions.question: What is happening in this scene?
options: a quiet morning walk in the field
the hare laughing at the tortoise
a loud fight between two birds
optionsZh: 田野裡安靜的早晨散步
questions.explanationZh: 這一幕是兔子在大家面前笑烏龜——這就是整個場景在發生的事。
questions.sentenceZh: 大兔子在全田野的面前嘲笑那隻小綠動物。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every day, the hare jumped fast across the open field and said "I am the fastest!" No animal said a word back.
questions.sentenceZh: 每天，兔子都在田野上嗖嗖嗖地跳，大聲說「我最快！」沒有動物回應。
questions.question: What does the hare do every day?
questions.explanationZh: 「每天跑很快」＋「我最快！」大聲說給大家聽——這就是「跑很快又愛現」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Look at me! I am the fastest!" he said loud to the other animals.
questions.sentenceZh: 「看我！我最快！」他大聲跟其他動物說。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare quiet and humble about his speed?
questions.explanationZh: 「看我！我最快！」說得又大聲又得意——哪裡謙虛？所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A tortoise walked by, slow and quiet, on the side of the path.
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻烏龜慢慢地、安靜地，沿著路邊走了過來。
questions.question: How did the tortoise move?
questions.explanationZh: 「slow and quiet」——慢慢地、安靜地，就是「慢慢安靜地走」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare bent down close to the tortoise's small head, still smiling.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子彎下身，湊近烏龜的小腦袋，臉上還掛著笑。
questions.question: Why did the hare smile when he bent close to the tortoise?
options: he was always last to finish
speed was never his strong point
optionsZh: 他總是最後一個完成的
速度從來不是他的強項
questions.explanationZh: 湊近又笑——那個笑不是友善，是「覺得烏龜可笑又弱小」，嘲弄的笑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise looked up at him. "I will race you," he said, slow and sure.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜慢慢抬起頭。「我來和你比賽，」他說，慢慢的、篤定的。
questions.questionEn: Was the tortoise nervous when he challenged the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 「慢慢的、篤定的」——不緊張、不慌張，沉穩地說出來，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the opening scene.
questions.sentenceZh: 把開場那段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是故事開場的關鍵片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal said a word back, and they all looked at the ground.
questions.sentenceZh: 沒有任何動物回話，大家都低著頭看地。
questions.question: What does the animals' silence tell us?
options: they were ready to race at dawn
everyone said the tortoise would lose
something big was about to change
optionsZh: 他們準備好在黎明比賽了
questions.explanationZh: 大家低頭、沒人回應——這說明「他們聽膩了他的自誇」，一點都不想搭理他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Step by step, his short legs moved without any sound.
questions.sentenceZh: 一步一步，他短短的腿沒有發出任何聲音。
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise move with loud, heavy steps?
questions.explanationZh: 「沒有任何聲音」——安靜極了，哪裡「大聲又沉重」？所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Hello, slow one," the hare laughed. "Can you even walk?"
questions.sentenceZh: 「嗨，慢慢仔！」兔子笑著說，「你這樣也叫走路？」
questions.question: How did the hare speak to the tortoise?
options: in a kind and friendly way
questions.explanationZh: 「慢慢仔！你這樣也叫走路？」這是嘲諷，就是「嘲弄地說」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every day, the hare jumped fast across the open field.
questions.sentenceZh: 每天，兔子都在空曠的田野上嗖嗖地跳。
questions.question: What was the hare's biggest problem?
options: he kept going step by step
pride made him stop to rest
optionsZh: 他一步一步繼續往前走
questions.explanationZh: 跑那麼快卻輸了——問題不是腿，是「太驕傲、太愛吹噓了」，驕傲讓他犯了大錯。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise looked up at him. "I will race you," he said, slow and sure...
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜慢慢抬起頭，看著兔子，不慌不忙地說：「我來和你比賽。」那股篤定，讓兔子愣了一下。

storyBeat: 比賽開始 — 差距這麼大,追得上嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 race = 比賽
🔑 tree = 樹
🔑 judge = 裁判
🔑 watch = 看
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare fell over laughing. "You? Race me?" he said again and again.
questions.explanationZh: 兔子笑到整個人倒在地上，肚子都笑痛了。「你？跟我比賽？」他重複了一遍又一遍，就是不相信。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His ears shook hard from his loud laughing.
questions.questionEn: Did the hare believe the tortoise could win?
questions.explanationZh: 笑到耳朵都在搖——這種笑法就是「完全不相信烏龜能贏」，所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 他笑得那麼大聲，連耳朵都搖起來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Yes. Tomorrow, at the big tree," the tortoise said quietly.
questions.explanationZh: 「對，明天，大樹那裡見。」烏龜安安靜靜說，不激動、不慌張，就是穩穩的一句話。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A clever fox said he would stand at the finish line and call the winner.
questions.question: What was the fox's job?
questions.explanationZh: 站在終點喊誰贏——這就是「裁判」的工作！
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻聰明的狐狸說，他要站在終點那裡，宣布誰贏。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The next morning, all the animals came to the field to watch.
questions.explanationZh: 隔天一早，田野裡擠滿了動物，大家都好奇：烏龜真的敢跟兔子比賽？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Who was at the race?
questions.question: Who was at the race?
questions.explanationZh: 所有動物都來了——田野上到處是大家，當然是「很多動物」！
questions.sentenceZh: 比賽現場有誰？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Ready, set, go!" the fox called out in a clear voice.
questions.explanationZh: 「預備——開始！」狐狸一聲喊，清清楚楚，比賽就這樣展開了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In one minute, the brown hare was a small dot ahead on the road.
questions.question: How fast did the hare go?
questions.explanationZh: 一分鐘就跑得只剩一個小點——這不是快，這是「非常非常快」！
questions.sentenceZh: 才一分鐘，棕色的兔子已經變成路上遠遠一個小點。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise lifted one foot, then put it down. Then the next foot. Then the next.
questions.question: Why did the tortoise still keep going?
questions.explanationZh: 一步一步穩穩地走，沒有停下來，沒有放棄——這說明他「有穩定的計畫」，慢慢往前走就對了。
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜抬起一隻腳，放下去。再下一隻腳，再下一隻腳。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare fell over laughing. "You? Race me?" he said again and again.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子笑到整個人倒在地上。「你？跟我比賽？」他一遍又一遍地說。
questions.question: How did the hare react to the tortoise's challenge?
options: he dashed ahead but stopped to sleep
he ran straight to the finish
the tortoise took the lead early
both animals ran at the same pace
optionsZh: 他衝出去、但停下來睡了一覺
questions.explanationZh: 笑到倒在地上，還一遍一遍地重複「你？」——就是「笑了，完全不相信」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Ready, set, go!" the fox ___ out in a clear voice.
questions.sentenceZh: 「預備——開始！」狐狸用清楚的聲音喊出來。
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh: 這是故事裡已經發生的事，所以用過去式。call 的過去式是 called！「the fox called out」——狐狸喊出來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Yes. Tomorrow, at the big tree," the tortoise said quietly.
questions.sentenceZh: 「對，明天，大樹那裡見，」烏龜安靜地說。
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise agree to race the next day?
questions.explanationZh: 「對，明天，大樹那裡見」——他同意了！明天比賽，所以答 Yes。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A clever fox said he would stand at the finish line and call the winner.
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻聰明的狐狸說，他要站在終點，宣布誰贏。
questions.question: What was the fox's role in the race?
options: he laughed at the tortoise for trying
the hare felt nervous about losing
the tortoise sped past without words
both animals cheered each other on
optionsZh: 他嘲笑烏龜居然來挑戰
野兔緊張地怕自己會輸
烏龜默默地超越了野兔
questions.explanationZh: 站在終點、宣布誰贏——這就是「終點的裁判」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The next morning, all the animals came to the field to watch.
questions.sentenceZh: 隔天早上，所有動物都來到田野觀看。
questions.questionEn: Did the race happen at night?
questions.explanationZh: 「the next morning」——是早上，不是晚上，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these race phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把比賽相關的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是比賽開始那一段的重要片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In one minute, the brown hare was a small dot ahead on the road.
questions.sentenceZh: 才一分鐘，棕色的兔子已經變成路上遠遠一個小點。
questions.question: What does this tell us about the hare's speed?
options: he took a long nap and lost
the hare won by one step
the tortoise gave up at the hill
both ended the race at the same time
questions.explanationZh: 一分鐘就變成遠遠的小點——這說明他「非常快」！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise lifted one foot, then put it down. Then the next foot. Then the next.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜抬起一隻腳，放下去。再下一隻腳，再下一隻腳。
questions.question: What does the tortoise's way of moving tell us?
options: he trusted his speed and grew careless
the hare trained all night before the race
his fear of losing kept him awake
the tortoise's steady pace worried him
optionsZh: 他太相信自己的速度、所以掉以輕心
野兔在比賽前整夜練習
對失敗的恐懼讓他睡不著
烏龜穩定的速度讓他擔心
questions.explanationZh: 一隻腳換一隻腳，沒有停——這說明「他穩穩地，不會停下來」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His ears shook hard from his loud laughing.
questions.sentenceZh: 他笑得那麼大聲，連耳朵都搖起來了。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare's laughter gentle and soft?
questions.explanationZh: 「耳朵都搖起來了」——這樣的笑聲是大聲的、誇張的，哪裡「輕柔」？答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare was already far away. The tortoise had taken only one step.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子早就跑到遠處了。烏龜才走了一步。
questions.question: At the start of the race, who was winning?
options: slow and steady still wins the race
speed is always the better choice
resting is fine whenever you want
optionsZh: 速度永遠是更好的選擇
想休息的時候隨時可以休息
打個盹從來沒傷害過任何人
questions.explanationZh: 兔子「遠遠跑走了」，烏龜才走一步——「兔子遠遠領先」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare was already far away. The tortoise had taken only one step...
questions.explanationZh: 兔子早就跑到不知道哪裡去了。烏龜呢？才走了一步……差距大得讓人心疼，但他還是走。

storyBeat: 兔子停下休息 — 他真的要睡?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 rest = 休息
🔑 sun = 太陽
🔑 warm = 暖
🔑 sleep = 睡
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare looked back. The tortoise was a tiny dot far away on the road.
questions.explanationZh: 兔子回頭一看，烏龜在路上只是個遠遠小小的點，好笑！這樣也能追上我？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He could not even see the tortoise's shell from this far ahead.
questions.questionEn: Was the gap between them small?
questions.explanationZh: 連殼都看不見了——距離大得很，哪裡「小」？所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 他離前面太遠了，連烏龜的殼都看不見。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I have time," the hare said to himself. "I will rest under this tree."
questions.explanationZh: 「我時間多的是，」兔子對自己說，「先在這棵樹下休息一下，等他來。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He thought there was no chance the small animal could ever pass him.
questions.question: Why did the hare want to rest?
questions.explanationZh: 覺得烏龜追不上自己，又跑在前面——他覺得「安全又領先」，所以才停下來休息。
questions.sentenceZh: 他心想，那隻小動物根本不可能追上他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The grass was soft. The sun was warm on his fur.
questions.explanationZh: 草地軟軟的，太陽暖洋洋地照著他的毛，舒服極了，眼皮開始變得好重……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What was the weather like?
questions.question: What was the weather like?
questions.explanationZh: 太陽曬得暖暖的——這就是「晴朗溫暖」的好天氣，最容易讓人想睡覺的那種！
questions.sentenceZh: 今天的天氣怎麼樣？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will wake up in a moment," he said with a long, slow yawn.
questions.explanationZh: 「我等一下就起來，」他說完，打了一個又長又慢的大呵欠——呼～呼～眼皮越來越沉了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His head dropped down onto his paws, soft and heavy.
questions.question: What was happening to the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 頭垂在腳上、重重的——這是「在睡著」！眼皮已經快蓋上了。
questions.sentenceZh: 他的頭垂在腳掌上，軟軟的、沉沉的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Far ahead of the race, the rabbit chose to take a nap under the shade.
questions.question: What is this scene mainly about?
questions.explanationZh: 這一幕說的就是：兔子跑在前面，卻決定睡覺。
questions.sentenceZh: 大幅領先的兔子，在樹蔭下選擇打個盹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare looked back. The tortoise was a tiny dot far away on the road.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子回頭一看。烏龜在遠遠的路上，只是個小點。
questions.question: What did the hare see when he looked back?
options: nothing — just an empty road behind
a tiny moving shape in the distance
the fox cheering at the finish line
the tortoise right beside the big tree
optionsZh: 什麼都沒有——只有後面空曠的路
遠方一個小小的移動形狀
questions.explanationZh: 「遠遠路上的小點」——這就是他看到的。他跑得太快，烏龜還在很遠的地方。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I have time," the hare said to himself. "I will rest under this tree."
questions.sentenceZh: 「我時間多的是，」兔子對自己說，「我要在這棵樹下休息一下。」
questions.questionEn: Was the hare worried about losing?
questions.explanationZh: 「我時間多的是」——他一點都不擔心，完全輕鬆，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The grass was soft. The sun was warm on his fur.
questions.sentenceZh: 草地軟軟的，太陽暖洋洋地照著他的毛。
questions.question: Why was it easy for the hare to fall asleep?
options: it was warm, soft and comfortable
it was dark and quiet at night
a friend sang him a song
questions.explanationZh: 軟草、暖陽——「又暖又軟又舒服」，這種環境最容易讓人睡著了！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will wake up in a moment," he said with a long, slow yawn.
questions.sentenceZh: 「我等一下就起來，」他說完，打了一個又長又慢的大呵欠。
questions.question: What did the hare plan to do?
options: rest for a short while and then run again
sleep until the race was over
give up the race and go home
wait for the tortoise to catch up
questions.explanationZh: 他說「等一下就起來」——他計畫只休息一下下，不知道自己會睡那麼久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He thought there was no chance the small animal could ever pass him.
questions.sentenceZh: 他心想，那隻小動物根本不可能追上他。
questions.questionEn: Did the hare think he could lose to the tortoise?
questions.explanationZh: 他覺得「根本不可能追上我」——完全沒有考慮輸的可能，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these nap scene phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把兔子睡覺那段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是兔子休息那段的關鍵片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His head dropped down onto his paws, soft and heavy.
questions.sentenceZh: 他的頭垂在腳掌上，軟軟的、沉沉的。
questions.question: What happened to the hare's head?
options: it proved slow and steady wins
resting in a race is fine
optionsZh: 這證明了穩紮穩打能贏
questions.explanationZh: 「頭垂在腳掌上」——是「睡著，頭垂了下去」的樣子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He could not even see the tortoise's shell from this far ahead.
questions.sentenceZh: 他離前面太遠了，連烏龜的殼都看不到。
questions.question: What is the hare's mistake in this scene?
options: he had wasted his lead sleeping
the tortoise ran faster that day
his longer legs gave him no advantage
optionsZh: 他把領先的優勢浪費在睡覺上了
他更長的腿沒有給他任何優勢
野兔的朋友分散了他的注意力
questions.explanationZh: 這一段的核心錯誤就是：「太自信」，覺得絕對不會輸，所以才敢睡——「太自信，決定睡覺」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He sat down on the soft grass. His eyes closed slowly.
questions.sentenceZh: 他在軟草地上坐下來，眼睛慢慢闔上了。
questions.questionEn: Did the hare fall asleep during the race?
questions.explanationZh: 「坐下來、眼睛慢慢閉上」——他真的睡著了，就在比賽中間，答 Yes！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Far ahead of the race, the rabbit chose to take a nap under the shade.
questions.sentenceZh: 大幅領先的兔子，選擇在樹蔭下打個盹。
questions.question: What is the main lesson from this part of the story?
options: being too confident can lead to mistakes
it is important to rest when you are tired
the tortoise was secretly very fast
questions.explanationZh: 兔子領先那麼多還是輸了——就是因為「太過自信導致錯誤」，這是這一段的核心教訓。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He sat down on the soft grass. His eyes closed slowly...
questions.explanationZh: 他在軟草地上坐下來，眼睛慢慢闔上，慢慢地……闔上了……整個田野只剩下風的聲音。

storyBeat: 烏龜走過 — 兔子要醒了嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 past = 走過
🔑 stop = 停
🔑 feet = 腳
🔑 sure = 確定
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise came up to the tree. He saw the sleeping hare.
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜慢慢走到樹下，看見兔子正閉著眼睛，呼嚕呼嚕地睡著了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His shell was hot from the long walk in the bright sun.
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise walk a long way?
questions.explanationZh: 殼都曬得熱熱的——這說明他走了很長、很遠的路，所以答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 在亮晃晃的太陽下走了這麼久，他的殼都曬熱了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "He is resting," the tortoise said quietly. "I will not stop."
questions.explanationZh: 「他在休息，」烏龜小聲說，「我不停。」說完，繼續往前走，一步都沒遲疑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He did not look at the rabbit's soft fur or his white teeth.
questions.question: What did the tortoise do?
options: stopped to wake the hare up
kept his eyes on the road
lay down for a rest too
questions.explanationZh: 不看兔子，眼睛只盯著前面的路——他就是「眼睛看著路」，專心往前走。
questions.sentenceZh: 他沒有看兔子柔軟的毛或白白的牙齒。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One foot moved. Then the other foot. Then the first one again.
questions.explanationZh: 一隻腳動了，換另一隻，再換第一隻……就這樣，一步接一步，沒有停過。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the tortoise keep moving?
questions.question: How did he keep moving?
questions.explanationZh: 一隻腳一隻腳輪流動——就是「一次一隻腳」，穩穩地走，不快、也不停。
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜是怎麼繼續前進的？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Behind him, the field was silent. The hare did not move at all.
questions.explanationZh: 他身後，田野靜悄悄的，兔子一動也不動地躺在樹下，耳朵貼著草地……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The brown ears stayed flat on the soft grass beside the tree.
questions.question: Was the hare still asleep?
questions.explanationZh: 耳朵平貼草地——還在熟睡，睡得很沉，所以答「是，睡得很沉」。
questions.sentenceZh: 棕色的耳朵平平地貼在樹旁的軟草上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise did not look back. He kept his eyes on the tree far ahead.
questions.question: Why did the tortoise not look back?
options: he could not turn his head
questions.explanationZh: 不回頭、眼睛只看前面——因為「他想保持專心」，不讓任何事情打擾他的步調。
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜沒有回頭看，他把眼睛放在前方那棵樹上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise came up to the tree. He saw the sleeping hare.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜慢慢走到樹下，看見兔子正在睡覺。
questions.question: What did the tortoise do when he saw the sleeping hare?
options: he woke up just as the tortoise won
the hare arrived first at the tree
optionsZh: 他醒來時烏龜剛好贏了
兩隻動物一起越過終點
questions.explanationZh: 「我不停」——烏龜說完，就繼續往前走，沒有停、沒有叫醒兔子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "He is resting," the tortoise said quietly. "I will not stop."
questions.sentenceZh: 「他在休息，」烏龜小聲說，「我不停。」
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise stop when he saw the hare sleeping?
questions.explanationZh: 「我不停」——說完繼續走，所以答 No！他沒有停。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One foot moved. Then the other foot. Then the first one again.
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻腳動了，換另一隻，再換第一隻。
questions.question: How did the tortoise keep going?
options: one steady step at a time
questions.explanationZh: 一隻腳、換另一隻、再換回來——就是「一次一步，穩穩地走」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He did not look at the rabbit's soft fur or his white teeth.
questions.sentenceZh: 他沒有看兔子柔軟的毛或白白的牙齒。
questions.question: What does this tell us about the tortoise?
options: he was ashamed of his careless choice
his pride grew even bigger after losing
the hare was happy to come in second
the animals gave him a second chance
optionsZh: 他為自己粗心的選擇感到羞愧
輸掉之後他的驕傲更大了
動物們給了他第二次機會
questions.explanationZh: 不看兔子——他「專注在自己的目標上」，不讓任何東西分心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The brown ears stayed flat on the soft grass beside the tree.
questions.sentenceZh: 棕色的耳朵平平地貼在樹旁的軟草上。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare awake when the tortoise passed him?
questions.explanationZh: 耳朵平貼著草——還在熟睡，根本沒醒，答 No！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the tortoise's walk.
questions.sentenceZh: 把烏龜走路那段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是烏龜堅持走完這段路的關鍵片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His feet moved one after the other, slow and sure.
questions.sentenceZh: 他的腳一隻接一隻，慢慢的、穩穩的。
questions.question: How would you describe the tortoise's way of walking?
questions.explanationZh: 「slow and sure」——慢慢穩穩地，就是「慢慢穩穩地」走法。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise came to the sleeping hare and walked past. He did not stop.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜走到睡著的兔子旁邊，就這樣走過去了。他沒有停下來。
questions.question: What is the contrast between the tortoise and hare in this scene?
options: the tortoise was scared but the hare was brave
the tortoise stayed focused while the hare slept
the hare worked hard but the tortoise was lazy
both animals decided to rest together
optionsZh: 烏龜很害怕，但兔子很勇敢
烏龜保持專注，而兔子在睡覺
兔子很努力，但烏龜很懶
兩隻動物決定一起休息
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜一步一步繼續走，兔子卻在睡覺——「烏龜保持專注，兔子在睡覺」是這一幕最大的對比。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise did not look back. He kept his eyes on the tree far ahead.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜沒有回頭看，他把眼睛放在前方那棵樹上。
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise look back to check on the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 「沒有回頭看」——清楚說了沒有，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Behind him, the field was silent. The hare did not move at all.
questions.sentenceZh: 他身後，田野靜悄悄的，兔子一動也不動。
questions.question: What is the main idea of this lesson?
options: the hare won the race by running fast
steady effort beats sleeping and being overconfident
the tortoise asked for help from his friends
the fox changed the rules of the race
optionsZh: 穩定的努力勝過睡覺和過度自信
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜穩穩走過去，兔子在睡——「穩定的努力勝過睡覺和過度自信」就是這節的主題。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His feet moved one after the other, slow and sure. The finish tree grew closer...
questions.explanationZh: 他的腳一隻接一隻，慢慢的、穩穩的。前方那棵大樹，越來越近了……就快到了！

storyBeat: 動物悄悄話 — 沒人叫醒他嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 animal = 動物
🔑 whisper = 悄悄話
🔑 wake = 醒
🔑 still = 仍然
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: On the side of the field, the watching animals began to move closer.
questions.explanationZh: 田野旁邊看比賽的動物們，開始悄悄靠近一點點——因為有什麼事情，正在悄悄發生……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small bird turned its head toward the sleeping rabbit and then away.
questions.questionEn: Were the animals looking at the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 小鳥把頭轉向兔子——有「在看他」嘛，所以答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻小鳥把頭轉向睡著的兔子，又轉開了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Look! The tortoise has passed him!" a small fox whispered.
questions.explanationZh: 「你看！烏龜已經超過他了！」一隻小狐狸忍不住，悄悄說出來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A mouse opened her mouth, then closed it without making a sound.
questions.question: Why did the mouse close her mouth?
options: she did not want to wake him
questions.explanationZh: 張嘴又閉上，忍住了——她想說、又不說，是因為「不想叫醒兔子」，讓比賽繼續公平進行。
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻老鼠張開嘴，又閉上了，沒有發出任何聲音。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal called out. No animal moved. The wind was the only sound.
questions.explanationZh: 沒有動物喊，沒有動物動，整個田野靜悄悄的，只有風輕輕吹過草的聲音。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How was the field?
questions.question: How was the field?
questions.explanationZh: 沒人喊、沒人動，只有風聲——這就是「非常安靜」，靜得讓人屏住呼吸。
questions.sentenceZh: 田野現在怎麼樣？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise was now closer to the big tree than to the sleeping hare.
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜離那棵大樹，比離睡著的兔子還近了——真的快到了！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The green back of the slow walker was the closest thing to the finish line.
questions.question: Who was closer to the finish?
questions.explanationZh: 綠色的背、走得慢——說的當然是烏龜！他現在是最靠近終點的「綠色小動物」。
questions.sentenceZh: 那隻慢慢走的綠色背影，是最靠近終點的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The animals watched the tortoise. None of them turned around to look at the hare.
questions.question: Why did no one wake the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 沒人去叫醒兔子——因為「他們想要公平比賽」，不想破壞規則。
questions.sentenceZh: 動物們看著烏龜，沒有一個轉身去看兔子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: On the side of the field, the watching animals began to move closer.
questions.sentenceZh: 田野旁邊看比賽的動物們，開始悄悄靠近一點點。
questions.question: Why did the animals move closer?
options: they wanted to join the race
it was getting dark and cold
optionsZh: 有令人驚訝的事情正在發生
questions.explanationZh: 靠過來——是因為「有令人驚訝的事情正在發生」：烏龜竟然超過兔子了！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise ___ now closer to the big tree than to the sleeping hare.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜現在離那棵大樹，比離睡著的兔子還近了。
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh: 這是故事裡已經發生的事，用過去式。the tortoise 是第三人稱單數，所以用 was！「The tortoise was closer」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal called out. No animal moved. The wind was the only sound.
questions.sentenceZh: 沒有動物喊，沒有動物動，只有風聲。
questions.questionEn: Did any animal make a noise to warn the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 「沒有動物喊出來」——沒有人去叫醒兔子，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Look! The tortoise has passed him!" a small fox whispered.
questions.sentenceZh: 「你看！烏龜已經超過他了！」一隻小狐狸悄悄說。
questions.question: What did the small fox notice?
options: the tortoise was now ahead of the hare
the hare was still in the lead
neither one could see the finish line
optionsZh: 烏龜現在已經在野兔前面了
questions.explanationZh: 「Look! The tortoise has passed him!」——小狐狸發現「烏龜已經超過兔子了」！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A mouse opened her mouth, then closed it without making a sound.
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻老鼠張開嘴，又閉上了，沒有發出任何聲音。
questions.questionEn: Did the mouse warn the hare?
questions.explanationZh: 她「張嘴又閉上，沒有出聲」——沒有警告兔子，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these whisper scene phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把動物悄悄話那段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是這一節的重點片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The green back of the slow walker was the closest thing to the finish line.
questions.sentenceZh: 那隻慢慢走的綠色背影，是最靠近終點的。
questions.question: Who was leading the race now?
options: the tortoise was closer than he thought
nobody came to watch that day
the fox moved the finish line
optionsZh: 烏龜比他以為的更近了
questions.explanationZh: 「綠色背影」是烏龜！他是「最靠近終點的」，代表他在領先。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The animals watched but no one woke the hare. They wanted a fair race.
questions.sentenceZh: 動物們在看，但沒有人去叫醒兔子。他們想要一場公平的比賽。
questions.question: Why was the animals' silence important?
options: a fair start mattered to everyone
the finish line was only for winners
speed was the only rule that counted
the winner could set the rules
optionsZh: 公平的開始對大家都很重要
速度是唯一算數的規則
questions.explanationZh: 沒有人去叫醒兔子——是因為「他們想讓比賽公平進行」，不想破壞規則。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then a young rabbit's voice broke the silence. "Is the hare still sleeping?"
questions.sentenceZh: 就在這時候，一隻小兔子的聲音打破了安靜：「兔子哥，還在睡嗎？」
questions.questionEn: Was it completely quiet until the end of this scene?
questions.explanationZh: 最後「一隻小兔子的聲音打破了安靜」——所以不是一直到結束都安靜的，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal called out. The wind was the only sound. The tortoise was now closer to the finish.
questions.sentenceZh: 沒有動物喊出來。只有風聲。烏龜現在離終點更近了。
questions.question: What is this scene mainly about?
options: step by step beats a quick nap
the fastest one always stops first
optionsZh: 最快的那個總是第一個停下來
questions.explanationZh: 全場安靜、只有風聲、烏龜越來越近——「緊張安靜地等待烏龜接近勝利」就是這幕的主旨。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then a young rabbit's voice broke the silence. "Is the hare still sleeping?"
questions.explanationZh: 就在這時候，一隻小小的兔子聲音打破了安靜：「兔子哥，還在睡嗎？」這下子，大家都聽到了。

storyBeat: 兔子醒了 — 來得及嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 open = 打開
🔑 road = 路
🔑 fly = 飛
🔑 wind = 風
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare opened his eyes. The sun was low in the sky.
questions.explanationZh: 兔子慢慢張開眼睛。太陽已經低低的了，不是早上了……他睡了多久？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long shadows from the trees stretched across the whole road.
questions.questionEn: Did the hare sleep for a short time?
questions.explanationZh: 太陽低了、樹影拉得老長——這說明已經是下午了，兔子睡了很久，不是「一小下」，所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 樹的長長影子，橫過了整條路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He looked down the road. His mouth fell open.
questions.explanationZh: 他往前面的路上看過去——嘴巴「啊」地張開，大大的，說不出話來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A tiny green shape was almost touching the big tree at the end.
questions.question: What did he see?
questions.explanationZh: 綠色小身影快碰到大樹——這就是「烏龜快到終點」了！要輸了！
questions.sentenceZh: 一個小小的綠色身影，幾乎要碰到盡頭的那棵大樹了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "No! No, no, no!" The hare jumped up from the grass.
questions.explanationZh: 「不！不不不！」兔子「咻」地從草地上彈起來，那股急，像被什麼燙到了！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the hare feel?
questions.question: How did the hare feel?
questions.explanationZh: 「不！不不不！」叫出來、跳起來——這不是開心也不是無聊，是「很慌張」！快要輸了！
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子的心情怎麼樣？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His legs flew as fast as they had ever moved.
questions.explanationZh: 他的腿動得比他這輩子任何時候都快，地都快飛起來了！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wind pushed his long ears flat behind his head.
questions.question: How fast was the hare running now?
questions.explanationZh: 風把耳朵都吹平了——這種速度，是「比以前任何時候都快」的那種！
questions.sentenceZh: 風把他長長的耳朵吹得平平地壓在頭後面。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare ran very fast, but the big tree was very close to the tortoise, not him.
questions.question: Could the hare win now?
options: yes, he can still win easily
no, the tortoise is too close
maybe, if he jumps very high
we cannot tell from the story
questions.explanationZh: 樹靠近烏龜、不靠近兔子——這說明「不，烏龜太靠近終點了」，兔子已經來不及了。
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子跑得很快，但那棵大樹是靠近烏龜，不是靠近他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare opened his eyes. The sun was low in the sky.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子慢慢張開眼睛。太陽已經低低的了。
questions.question: What does the low sun tell us?
options: it was the end of a very long road
the race had just barely started
everybody was still at the start line
optionsZh: 那是一段很長的路的終點
那天沒有發生什麼特別的事
questions.explanationZh: 太陽低低的——就快落山了，這是「傍晚了」的訊號。兔子睡了太久！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long shadows from the trees stretched across the whole road.
questions.sentenceZh: 樹的長長影子，橫過了整條路。
questions.questionEn: Was it still early morning when the hare woke up?
questions.explanationZh: 「長長的影子橫過整條路」——這是傍晚才有的景象，不是早上，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "No! No, no, no!" The hare jumped up from the grass.
questions.sentenceZh: 「不！不不不！」兔子從草地上跳起來。
questions.question: Why did the hare say "No! No, no, no!"?
options: a hard lesson he would never forget
a moment of luck and nothing more
optionsZh: 一個他永遠不會忘記的痛苦教訓
沒有什麼真正值得記住的
只是又一個普通的比賽日
只是運氣的瞬間，僅此而已
questions.explanationZh: 他看到綠色小身影快碰到大樹——「烏龜快到終點了」，讓他嚇得大喊「不！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A tiny green shape was almost touching the big tree at the end.
questions.sentenceZh: 一個小小的綠色身影，幾乎要碰到盡頭的那棵大樹了。
questions.question: What does the tiny green shape mean for the race?
options: the tortoise was nearly at the finish
a new animal had joined the race
the judge had moved the finish line
questions.explanationZh: 綠色身影快碰到樹了——「綠色身影」是烏龜，代表「烏龜快到終點了」！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His legs flew as fast as they had ever moved.
questions.sentenceZh: 他的腿動得比他這輩子任何時候都快。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare running slower than usual after waking up?
questions.explanationZh: 「比他這輩子任何時候都快」——是他最快的時候，當然不是「比平常慢」，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these wake-up panic phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把兔子驚醒慌張那段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是兔子慌張醒來那段的關鍵片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wind pushed his long ears flat behind his head.
questions.sentenceZh: 風把他長長的耳朵吹得平平地壓在頭後面。
questions.question: What does the wind on his ears show?
options: he had learned patience always pays off
his speed would serve him better next time
the loss was purely bad luck
optionsZh: 他學到了耐心終究有回報
下次他的速度一定會讓他贏
questions.explanationZh: 風把耳朵吹平了——只有跑得「非常快」的時候，風才會這樣把耳朵吹平。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare ran with all his strength. But the tortoise was at the big tree.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子拚盡全力跑。但烏龜已經到了大樹旁邊了。
questions.question: What is the tension in this scene?
options: the finish line was crossed with quiet pride
the hare arrived before anyone cheered
nobody was there to see the ending
the fox fell asleep before the race ended
optionsZh: 終點線在安靜的驕傲中被越過了
野兔在任何人歡呼之前就到了
沒有人在那裡看到結局
狐狸在比賽結束前睡著了
questions.explanationZh: 兔子拚命跑，但烏龜已經在樹旁了——「已經來不及了」，這就是這幕的緊張感。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He looked down the road. His mouth fell open.
questions.sentenceZh: 他往前面的路上看過去——嘴巴「啊」地張開，大大的。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare calm when he looked down the road?
questions.explanationZh: 嘴巴「啊」地大張——那是震驚的反應，不是「冷靜」，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare ran very fast, but the big tree was very close to the tortoise, not him.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子跑得很快，但那棵大樹是靠近烏龜，不是靠近他。
questions.question: What lesson can we learn from the hare's mistake?
options: do not let pride make you careless
slow animals can never win races
optionsZh: 跑得快的人永遠不能休息
不要讓驕傲讓你變粗心
慢的動物永遠贏不了比賽
裁判應該停掉不公平的比賽
questions.explanationZh: 兔子因為驕傲睡覺而輸——「不要讓驕傲讓你變粗心」就是這個故事教我們的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare ran with all his strength. But the tortoise was at the big tree...
questions.explanationZh: 兔子拚盡全力跑，每一步都用上了——但烏龜，已經到了大樹旁邊了……

storyBeat: 慢而穩 — 兔子學到了嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的最後一組單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 last = 最後
🔑 touch = 碰
🔑 breath = 呼吸
🔑 steady = 穩
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise lifted one short leg for the last time.
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜最後一次抬起那雙短短的腿，輕輕放下——他到了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His foot came down right at the bottom of the big tree.
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise reach the finish?
questions.explanationZh: 腳踩在樹根那裡——他到了終點了！所以答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 他的腳踩在大樹的樹根旁邊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The fox lifted a paw. "The tortoise wins!" he called out.
questions.explanationZh: 狐狸舉起腳，大聲宣布：「烏龜贏了！」這一聲，整個田野都聽見了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every animal at the field began to cheer and stamp their feet.
questions.question: How did the animals feel?
questions.explanationZh: 大家一起歡呼又跺腳——「興奮又開心」，為這位慢慢走卻贏了的烏龜鼓掌！
questions.sentenceZh: 在田野上的每一隻動物，都開始歡呼、用腳踩地。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare arrived a moment later. He could not even speak.
questions.explanationZh: 兔子晚了一步才到，站在那裡，連說「我輸了」都說不出口……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Why could the hare not speak?
questions.question: Why could the hare not speak?
questions.explanationZh: 剛拚命跑完，氣都喘不過來了——所以是「喘到沒氣」，根本說不了話。
questions.sentenceZh: 為什麼兔子說不出話來？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His chest moved up and down very fast. His mouth was open.
questions.explanationZh: 他的胸口上下起伏得很快，嘴巴張著——跑了那麼快、那麼久，當然喘得說不出話。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The brown rabbit pressed his front paws on the ground and looked at the dirt.
questions.question: How did the hare feel?
questions.explanationZh: 低著頭，看著地——他沒有說話，也不敢看別人，這就是「不好意思」，輸了很難受。
questions.sentenceZh: 棕色的兔子把前腳壓在地上，低頭看著泥土。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise turned his head to the hare and smiled gently.
questions.question: Why did the tortoise smile gently?
options: he was still angry at the hare
he wanted to share a lesson kindly
he wanted to laugh at him
he was tired after the long race
optionsZh: 他想溫和地分享一個道理
questions.explanationZh: 輕輕地微笑，不是嘲笑、不是報復——烏龜想溫和地說出這個道理，讓兔子自己明白。
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜把頭轉向兔子，輕輕微笑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise lifted one short leg for the last time. His foot came down right at the bottom of the big tree.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜最後一次抬起那雙短短的腿，腳踩在大樹的樹根旁邊。
questions.question: What happened when the tortoise reached the big tree?
options: he stopped and waited for the hare
the fox moved the finish line
questions.explanationZh: 腳踩在樹根——他到了終點！他「贏得了比賽」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The fox lifted a paw. "The tortoise wins!" he called out.
questions.sentenceZh: 狐狸舉起腳，大聲宣布：「烏龜贏了！」
questions.questionEn: Did the fox announce the hare as the winner?
questions.explanationZh: 狐狸說「烏龜贏了！」不是「兔子贏了！」，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hare arrived a moment later. He could not even speak.
questions.sentenceZh: 兔子晚了一步才到，連說話都說不出來。
questions.question: How did the hare feel when he arrived after the tortoise?
options: resting at the start helps you win
optionsZh: 穩定的努力勝過耀眼的速度
才能永遠比努力更重要
在起跑點休息幫助你贏
最快的那個永遠最先到
questions.explanationZh: 說不出話、低頭看地——「不好意思又喘不過氣」，輸得很難受。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every animal at the field began to cheer and stamp their feet.
questions.sentenceZh: 在田野上的每一隻動物，都開始歡呼、用腳踩地。
questions.question: How did the watching animals react to the tortoise's win?
options: they came to see who would win
only the fox watched from above
optionsZh: 動物們待在遠遠的地方
questions.explanationZh: 「歡呼又跺腳」——大家都為烏龜開心！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Slow and steady wins the race," the tortoise said softly.
questions.sentenceZh: 「慢而穩，就會贏，」烏龜輕輕說。
questions.questionEn: Did the tortoise say this with a loud, proud voice?
questions.explanationZh: 「softly」——他輕輕地說，不是大聲炫耀，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these ending phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把故事結尾的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是故事結尾最重要的片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The brown rabbit pressed his front paws on the ground and looked at the dirt.
questions.sentenceZh: 棕色的兔子把前腳壓在地上，低頭看著泥土。
questions.question: What was the hare doing with his head?
options: turning to look at the tortoise
questions.explanationZh: 「低頭看著泥土」——「低著頭，羞愧地」就是這個樣子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tortoise turned his head to the hare and smiled gently.
questions.sentenceZh: 烏龜把頭轉向兔子，輕輕微笑。
questions.question: What did the tortoise's smile show?
options: he raced with full heart from start to end
his speed made him stop and rest
the tortoise ran faster in the second half
a sudden storm helped the tortoise win
optionsZh: 他從頭到尾全力以赴地比賽
他的速度讓他停下來休息
烏龜在後半段跑得更快
一場突如其來的暴風雨幫助了烏龜
questions.explanationZh: 輕輕地微笑——不是嘲笑，是「善意」，他不想傷害兔子，只是想分享這個道理。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His chest moved up and down very fast. His mouth was open.
questions.sentenceZh: 他的胸口上下起伏得很快，嘴巴張著。
questions.questionEn: Was the hare relaxed when he arrived at the finish?
questions.explanationZh: 胸口快速起伏、嘴巴張著——他在喘氣，根本沒有放鬆，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Slow and steady wins the race," the tortoise said softly. The hare did not look up.
questions.sentenceZh: 「慢而穩，就會贏，」烏龜輕輕說。兔子沒有抬頭……
questions.question: What is the main message of the Tortoise and Hare story?
options: fast is always better than slow
steady effort and focus beat speed and pride
only strong animals can win races
it is wrong to challenge someone faster than you
optionsZh: 穩定的努力和專注，勝過速度和驕傲
只有強壯的動物才能贏比賽
挑戰比你快的人是錯的
questions.explanationZh: 烏龜慢但穩定，兔子快但驕傲——「穩定的努力和專注，勝過速度和驕傲」就是這個故事的核心訊息。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Slow and steady wins the race," the tortoise said softly. The hare did not look up...
questions.explanationZh: 「慢而穩，就會贏，」烏龜輕輕說。兔子沒有抬頭……但心裡，大概已經記住了這句話。
